Edexcel.name: Paper 2
Edexcel.title: Period Study and British Depth Study

assessmentObjectives.description: Recall, select and organise historical knowledge
Explain and analyse historical events and periods
Analyse and evaluate historical sources and interpretations

topics.name: Kaiser Wilhelm and Germany
topics.description: Kaiser Wilhelm II, the growth of constitutional government, the influence of Prussia, the army and the Navy Laws

topics.name: The Weimar Republic
topics.description: The establishment of the Weimar Republic, the Constitution, challenges from left and right, the Spartacist uprising and Kapp Putsch

topics.name: Hyperinflation and the Ruhr Crisis
topics.description: The occupation of the Ruhr, hyperinflation of 1923, impacts on different social groups, and the recovery under Stresemann

topics.name: The Golden Years: Stresemann
topics.description: Stresemann's policies: currency reform, Dawes Plan, Locarno Pact, League of Nations entry, cultural developments in Weimar

topics.name: The Rise of the Nazis
topics.description: The impact of the Wall Street Crash, the 1930 and 1932 elections, the role of Hitler, the SA, propaganda and the appointment as Chancellor

topics.name: Nazi Consolidation of Power
topics.description: Reichstag Fire, Enabling Act, Night of the Long Knives, death of Hindenburg, creation of the Fuhrer state

topics.name: Nazi Economic Policies
topics.description: Reducing unemployment, the Reich Labour Service, the Strength Through Joy programme, rearmament, the impact on workers

topics.name: Nazi Racial Policy and the Holocaust
topics.description: Persecution of minorities, the Nuremberg Laws, Kristallnacht, ghettos, the Final Solution, and opposition to Nazi rule

topics.name: Youth and Education in Nazi Germany
topics.description: Hitler Youth, the League of German Maidens, control of education, the Nazi curriculum, and resistance from the Edelweiss Pirates

topics.name: Women and the Family in Nazi Germany
topics.description: Nazi views on women's role, the Three Ks, marriage loans, Mother's Cross, and the impact of war on women's employment

strands.name: Conflict and Tension: The Inter-War Years, 1918-1939

topics.name: The Treaty of Versailles
topics.description: The Big Three, terms of the treaty (territorial, military, financial, war guilt), German reactions, and the establishment of the League of Nations

topics.name: The League of Nations
topics.description: The Covenant, membership problems, the Assembly and Council, the Permanent Court, successes in the 1920s (Aaland Islands, Greece-Bulgaria)

topics.name: The League in the 1930s
topics.description: Manchuria (1931), Abyssinia (1935-36), the impact of the Great Depression, disarmament failures, and the collapse of the League

topics.name: Hitler's Foreign Policy
topics.description: Rearmament, the Saar, remilitarisation of the Rhineland, Anschluss with Austria, the Sudetenland and the Munich Agreement

topics.name: Appeasement
topics.description: The reasons for appeasement, Chamberlain's policy, the Munich Agreement, the occupation of Czechoslovakia, and the end of appeasement

topics.name: The Road to War
topics.description: The Nazi-Soviet Pact, the invasion of Poland, the declaration of war, and the collapse of international peace by 1939

strands.name: Britain: Health and the People, c1000 to the Present Day

topics.name: Medicine in Medieval England
topics.description: Religious and rational approaches, Hippocrates and Galen, the Church and medicine, hospitals, the Black Death and responses

topics.name: The Renaissance and Medicine
topics.description: Vesalius, Paré, Harvey, the printing press, the Royal Society, and the Great Plague of 1665

topics.name: The Fight Against Disease
topics.description: Jenner and vaccination, Pasteur and germ theory, Koch, Lister and antiseptics, the development of pharmaceuticals and penicillin

topics.name: Surgery and Anaesthetics
topics.description: The problem of pain: Simpson and chloroform, the problem of infection: Lister and carbolic acid, the problem of bleeding: blood transfusions

topics.name: Public Health in the 19th Century
topics.description: Cholera epidemics, Edwin Chadwick, the 1848 and 1875 Public Health Acts, John Snow and the Broad Street pump

topics.name: The Liberal Reforms
topics.description: Booth and Rowntree's surveys, the 1906 Liberal government, old age pensions, National Insurance, free school meals, the People's Budget

topics.name: The NHS and the Welfare State
topics.description: The Beveridge Report, the establishment of the NHS in 1948, Aneurin Bevan, opposition from doctors, the impact of the welfare state

topics.name: Modern Medicine and Health
topics.description: The development of the NHS, advances in technology (X-rays, scanners), transplant surgery, antibiotics crisis, and 21st century challenges

topics.name: Factors in Medical Progress
topics.description: The role of individuals, war, government, chance, communication, and science and technology in driving medical change across the period

topics.name: Elizabeth's Background and Accession
topics.description: Elizabeth's early life, the religious settlement, challenges to the settlement, the role of the Church of England

topics.name: Challenges at Home and Abroad
topics.description: The Revolt of the Northern Earls, the Ridolfi, Throckmorton and Babington Plots, Mary Queen of Scots, and the execution 1587

topics.name: The Spanish Armada
topics.description: Causes of conflict with Spain, the Netherlands, Drake's raids, the Armada of 1588, reasons for English victory, and consequences

topics.name: Education and the Elizabethan World
topics.description: Education for boys and girls, the grammar schools, universities, the role of the theatre, the puritan challenge to entertainment

topics.name: The Problem of the Poor
topics.description: Causes of poverty, attitudes to the poor, the 1572 and 1601 Poor Laws, vagabonds and vagrancy, the treatment of the deserving and undeserving poor

topics.name: Exploration and the New World
topics.description: Drake's circumnavigation, Raleigh and Virginia, the reasons for exploration, the impact on indigenous peoples, and mercantilism

topics.name: The Historic Environment
topics.description: A specified site for study each year — may include country houses, theatres, or other Elizabethan buildings, their purpose and significance
